summary: Sprint 3 of Media Master ecosystem verified end-to-end. Backend: 13/13 new pytest cases pass for /tv/program, /tv/now-playing, AI Scout cf_input_id integration, and /api/media-master-pulse/snapshot. Regression shield 367/367 green. Frontend: /dsg-tv/arena renders Off-Air state with correct testid; /admin/media-master-pulse renders all 6 sections (4 KPI tiles + Hottest Rooms + Radio Bid Pools + Channel Revenue + Sponsor Leaderboard + Recent Clips). Critical privacy property verified: /tv/now-playing returns live:false and hides hls_playback_url when CF live input is_live=false. Pulse snapshot contains no Mongo _id leaks.

minor.issue: Preview env has CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN configured, so non-stub cf_input_id values that don't exist on CF cause the clipper to receive a 404 HTML response which lands as cf_status containing the raw HTML text (e.g. '404 page not found'). cf_stream_clipper.py line 96-98 returns resp.text[:200] verbatim when JSON parse fails. Cosmetic — playback_url stays None, status is non-rendered. Consider returning a normalized reason like 'cf_http_404' for cleaner downstream UI.

action_items: (Optional polish) Normalize cf_status reason strings in cf_stream_clipper when CF returns non-JSON HTML errors — currently leaks the raw HTML snippet into the clip doc.

(Optional) Index media_tv_channel_programs on (channel_id, programmed_until) — current _resolve_channel_live_input does a sort on programmed_at without an index; fine at preview scale, will matter at load.
critical_code_review_comments: _resolve_channel_live_input correctly enforces the privacy property: even with an active program row, if the underlying cf_live_inputs.is_live is False it returns None, so /tv/now-playing exposes no HLS URL. Test verified by flipping is_live=true and observing the URL appear. SAFETY VERIFIED.
_resolve_channel_live_input projects out rtmps_key and srt_passphrase from cf_live_inputs — good — those are streamer-only secrets.
media_master_pulse.py uses {_id: 0} projection in every find_one/find call and in the aggregate pipeline output is reshaped — no _id leak verified in raw response body.

ProgramRequest validates duration_hours via Pydantic Field(ge=1, le=72) — good. But duration_hours has default=4 even though the test req sent 4 explicitly; verify the default actually triggers when omitted (not blocking).

cf_stream_clipper.clip_live_input never raises — graceful no-op pattern is correct. Stub input short-circuit at line 67 is critical to prevent preview env from burning CF quota on synthetic IDs. VERIFIED working.

Pulse channel_revenue aggregation matches on exact reason string 'DSG TV unlock · {name}'. If unlock endpoint ever renames a channel, lifetime totals silently zero out. Consider tagging ledger rows with channel_id directly rather than free-text matching.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (both target pages render with correct testids)

seed_data_creation: Tests insert TEST_input_<uuid> CF live input docs into cf_live_inputs and TEST_room_<uuid> hype rows. test_program_valid_input_returns_record_with_4h_window cleans up its own rows. Other rows (TEST_room_* hype snapshots and clips) are left in test_database for inspection but use TEST_ prefix.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run: `cd /app/backend && python -m pytest tests/test_media_master_sprint3_jan2026.py tests/regression_shield.py -v`. The Sprint 3 tests rely on /app/backend/.env CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN being configured — preview env has it set. To simulate a true 'no CF creds' env, unset the token and the bogus-input test would shift from cf_http_404 to cf_not_configured. Test isolation: test_program_valid_input cleans up after itself; AI Scout tests rely on minute_bucket idempotency (one auto-clip per room per minute), so re-running within 60s for the same TEST_room won't mint a 2nd clip — that's by design.
verified_features: GET /tv/now-playing/arena returns {live:false, live_input:null} when no program
POST /tv/program with valid channel+input -> 200, program record, programmed_until ~4h ahead
After /tv/program, while is_live=false: /tv/now-playing still returns live=false, live_input=null (PRIVACY SHIELD VERIFIED)
After flipping cf_live_inputs.is_live=true: /tv/now-playing returns live=true with hls_playback_url
POST /scout/ingest with cf_input_id='stub_xyz' -> cf_status='stub_input' (no CF API call)
POST /scout/ingest without cf_input_id -> clip persisted with cf_clip_uid=null, playback_url=null
POST /scout/ingest with real-looking cf_input_id while CF token configured -> cf_status != 'rendered', playback_url=null for bogus input
Frontend /dsg-tv/arena renders dsg-tv-player-offline testid with 'Off air · The Arena · No streamer is broadcasting' copy

Frontend /admin/media-master-pulse renders media-master-pulse-page with pulse-kpis, pulse-hottest-rooms, pulse-station-pools, pulse-channel-revenue, pulse-sponsor-leaderboard, pulse-recent-clips testids and live data
